screening_06.selectedOption: Strong match — this is exactly what I want

screening_10.textValue: Asked about the LLM integration architecture and how research algorithms get productionized

phoneScreen.notes: Excellent communicator. Very articulate about past projects. Currently at a health-tech startup, so domain overlap is strong.

questions.responseSummary: Designed clear request/response Pydantic models. Suggested using background tasks for LLM calls with polling. Knew about FastAPI's BackgroundTasks.
questions.notes: Solid approach. Mentioned using httpx with timeout param. Knew about worker pool implications.

questions.responseSummary: Clearly explained event loop blocking with sync code in async endpoints. Gave a real example of a sync DB call blocking the entire loop.
questions.notes: Had a real war story about sync ORM calls inside async handlers. Knew to use run_in_executor as a workaround.

questions.responseSummary: Proposed a middleware that checks the issuer claim to route to the right validator. Discussed JWKS endpoint caching.
questions.notes: Solid JWT knowledge. Mentioned RS256 without prompting. Slightly vague on refresh token rotation.

questions.responseSummary: Described idempotency key stored in DB with unique constraint. Addressed the crash-after-process scenario — suggested processing and acking in a transaction.
questions.notes: Exceptional answer. Clearly has built webhook handlers before. Mentioned Terra API specifically which shows he researched us.

questions.responseSummary: Described standard unit/integration split. Knew to mock external APIs. Struggled a bit on what NOT to test.
questions.notes: Adequate but not deep. Testing is his weaker area. Would need mentoring here.

questions.responseSummary: Systematic approach: check monitoring, check recent infra changes, check DB connections, check external dependencies. Mentioned checking if certs expired.
questions.notes: Good systematic thinker. Mentioned real tools (Sentry, Grafana). The cert expiry angle was a nice practical touch.

impressions.redFlags: Testing depth is below what I'd like for mid-level
impressions.greenFlags: Excellent communicator. Domain overlap with health-tech. Researched our stack before the interview. Genuine enthusiasm.

fraudAssessment.notes: All answers felt authentic. Had specific stories and tool names. No suspicious pauses.
